title: The Messiah's Path: A Historical Geography from Bethlehem to Golgotha

ebookIntro.preTitle: Step out of the abstract. Walk the true roads of the Scriptures.
ebookIntro.sectionTitle: Welcome to the tangible reality of the Ancient Middle East.
ebookIntro.paragraph1: Do you feel your biblical reading sometimes lacks the physical context of the events? Do you wish to truly understand the distances, the difficult terrains, and the political powder keg that Jesus and his disciples faced daily?
ebookIntro.paragraph2: The Christian faith is not founded on abstract myths, but on real soil, dust, and stones. This guide acts as a time machine, transforming your reading from the abstract plane and bringing it into the tangible reality of the first century.
ebookIntro.highlightText: In this chronological journey, you will master:

benefits.title: The Compass (Maps and Terrains):
benefits.description: Understand the real distances and geographical challenges, from the isolated hills of Nazareth to the crushing heat of the Judean Desert.

benefits.title: The Evidence (Archaeology):
benefits.description: Discover the historical context that pop culture forgets. Learn about the political significance of the cities, first-century architecture, and cultural tensions.

benefits.title: The Vision (Window to the Past):
benefits.description: Visualize the biblical narrative through high-realism conceptual images that transport you directly to the fishing boats of Galilee and the shadows of Gethsemane.

ebookIntro.content:
Imagine a land suffocated by iron and dust. In the first century, the region of Judea was not a peaceful fairytale setting, but a true political and social powder keg under the relentless weight of the Roman Empire.
The focus of the Messiah's arrival was not on gleaming marble palaces, but on the vulnerability of a limestone manger and the obscurity of small farming villages.
This e-book was designed to be a time machine. It will transform your biblical reading, taking it from the abstract plane and bringing it into the tangible reality of the Ancient Middle East.
To ensure a complete and immersive experience, this journey is divided chronologically. At every stop — from the humble origins in Nazareth to the glory of the Empty Tomb — you will explore the exact maps, read the central biblical records, and discover the historical and archaeological evidence that brings the text to life.
You will learn that Jesus did not shy away from the harshness of the world. He climbed steep hills under the scorching sun, smelled the fish in the harbors of Capernaum, and faced the crushing silence of the desert. Biblical geography teaches us that God works in the details of time and space.
Prepare yourself. The journey is about to begin. Put on your sandals; the road is long, and the dust of the Galilean paths awaits us.
ebookIntro.ctaText: To continue this journey and discover the deep historical roots of the Scriptures, acquire full access.
ebookIntro.description: A visual and historical guide that transports your biblical study to the real roads, cities, and archaeological context of first-century Judea and Galilee.
ebookIntro.subtitle: Discover the Soil, Dust, and Stones of the Gospels
